skill.name: vision-exploration
skill.description: Guides open-ended product vision exploration from a vague idea to multiple future-state HTML concepts.
skill.summary: Explore the farthest possible product end states before narrowing design direction.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported six issues, but the external command, weak cryptography, and entropy findings are false positives after context review. The inline backticks at SKILL.md:130 describe a Markdown path, the weak-crypto hits are frontmatter or design-related words, and the entropy signal is explained by Chinese Markdown text. The retained risk is low because the skill asks the agent to create and archive HTML files under a user-confirmed directory.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Output Requires User Review
low_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to create 4-6 single-file HTML outputs and archive results under a user-confirmed directory. This is a normal design workflow, but it can write files in the workspace, so users should confirm paths and filenames.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing behavior is explicitly described in the workflow. Risk is low because the skill asks the user to confirm the directory and filenames.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md:130 is a false positive. The backticks wrap a directory path in Markdown and do not indicate shell, Ruby, or command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line contains only inline Markdown formatting for a path. No command syntax, executable code, or user input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Entropy Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:8, SKILL.md:9, and SKILL.md:106 are false positives from frontmatter and design-related wording. The entropy heuristic at SKILL.md:1 is also a false positive; the scanned file is plain Markdown with Chinese text, not binary or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, encoded payload, binary content, or obfuscated code. The flagged locations are ordinary prose and YAML frontmatter.

content.value_statement: Early product ideas can feel too vague to judge or communicate. This skill guides value discovery, motivation mapping, evolution planning, and polished future-state HTML concepts.
actual_capabilities: Guides users from a vague idea toward a clear value statement.
Prompts for real user motivations before proposing future directions.
Builds a natural evolution path from simple needs to long-term product forms.
Generates 4-6 distinct future-state HTML concepts with inline CSS.
Compares each concept by core idea, use case, and information architecture.
Archives exploration outputs after confirming directory and file names with the user.
limitations: It explores ambitious possibilities and does not validate technical feasibility.
It needs user answers at key steps to avoid guessing motivations.
The HTML outputs are concept artifacts, not production-ready applications.
It does not include external market research unless the user provides source material.

use_cases.title: Shape a Product Vision
use_cases.description: Turn a rough feature idea into several long-term product directions before roadmap planning.

use_cases.title: Prepare Investor Storytelling
use_cases.description: Create polished future-state concepts that show how a simple idea could become a larger platform.

use_cases.title: Expand Design Exploration
use_cases.description: Move beyond layout variations by comparing different interaction models and information structures.

prompt_templates.title: Start From A Rough Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a rough idea: [describe idea]. Help me explore the value, user motivations, evolution path, and possible end states.

prompt_templates.title: Explore A Feature Future
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for an existing feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use vision-exploration on this feature: [feature]. Start by challenging the value, then guide me through motivations and future-state options.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Different End States
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for choosing among strategic directions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore 4-6 end-state visions for [product area]. Make each one use a different interaction model and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Board-Ready Concepts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for high-polish concept artifacts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete vision exploration for [initiative]. Produce polished HTML concepts, a comparison table, and an archive structure after confirming names with me.

output_examples.input: A team wants a simple page for switching between AI API providers.
output: Value statement: the real need is AI resource management, not provider switching.
Evolution path: manual switching, informed switching, proactive alerts, then automated resource orchestration.
Concept directions: event stream, control room, conversational assistant, budget timeline, and autonomous policy dashboard.

output_examples.input: A founder wants to explore a lightweight customer feedback widget.
output: The skill first asks why users leave feedback and what teams do after receiving it.
It then maps future paths such as insight routing, decision memory, and automated product opportunity discovery.
The final package includes multiple HTML vision concepts and a clear comparison of their product logic.

output_examples.input: A designer wants more than layout variants for a reporting dashboard.
output: The skill reframes the dashboard around decisions, risk signals, team routines, and time-based narratives.
It produces distinct interface visions instead of list, grid, and table variations.

best_practices: Start with a real but unfinished idea, not a fully specified solution.
Answer motivation questions with concrete user situations and constraints.
Review the evolution path before asking for final HTML concepts.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you only need a production implementation plan.
Do not skip value discovery and ask for screens immediately.
Do not treat small layout changes as separate strategic directions.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a value framing, user motivation list, evolution path, multiple future-state HTML concepts, and a comparison summary.

faq.question: Is this skill for implementation work?
faq.answer: No. It is for strategic exploration and concept design before implementation planning.

faq.question: Can it work with a vague idea?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is designed to start from a vague seed and ask clarifying questions.

faq.question: Does it create production-ready HTML?
faq.answer: No. The HTML files are polished concept artifacts for exploration and communication.

faq.question: Why does it ask for confirmations?
faq.answer: Confirmations keep the exploration grounded in the user's real goals before moving to the next stage.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no malicious code, network behavior, command execution, or prompt injection attempt. The only retained concern is normal file output.
